source: a16z Podcast — Is there an AI bubble?
summary: Consumer AI business model will shift to outcome-based affiliate fees, squeezing out Google's advertising inefficiency premium
summary_extended: Baker argues that the dominant business model for consumer AI assistants will shift from advertising to outcome-based affiliate fees, fundamentally disrupting Google's core search advertising franchise. His reasoning is that Google's advertising model generates excess returns precisely because advertisers systematically overpay for customer acquisition — a structural inefficiency that AI can eliminate by closing the loop between intent and transaction (e.g., an AI assistant that books a hotel on a user's behalf and earns a fee only upon completion). This shift would compress the 'inefficiency premium' embedded in Google's ad revenue, representing a meaningful degradation of its business model over time. Baker notes that his personal preferred AI assistant is XAI/Grok, which he discloses is influenced by his direct shareholding in XAI, though XAI is not publicly listed. The investment implication is bearish on GOOGL's long-term advertising revenue durability, though Baker hedges on timing.
detail: Baker expects AI assistants to negotiate on behalf of users (e.g., hotel bookings) and earn affiliate fees for closed-loop outcomes. This is a business model degradation vs. Google's advertising model, which works because advertisers systematically overpay for customer acquisition. AI closing the loop will squeeze out that inefficiency. XAI/Grok is his personal preferred AI assistant given his XAI shareholding.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as directionally likely but uses hedged language about timing
quote_fragment: affiliate fees... being paid for an outcome

ticker_audit.reasoning: The thesis is explicitly about the disruption of Google's advertising business model by outcome-based AI affiliate fees. GOOGL is the direct subject of the structural threat Baker describes — its ad revenue depends on the inefficiency premium that AI closes out.
